commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Chaldeans. Nabuchodonosor was the first of this nation who attacked Joakim, and having conquered all as far as the Nile, returned to succeed Nabopolassar. He afterwards came upon Jechonias and Sedecias The prophet might have all this in view, particularly the first invasion. (Calmet)
Bitter; warlike, as all the Greek historians remark. (St. Jerome)
The Chaldeans were not yet arrived at such greatness, and of course this is not the Habacuc specified Daniel xiv. (Worthington)
Yet the same prophet might foresee it. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
